title: Unit, Property, Replay, Headless SDL, Render, and Playtest Evidence · Mastery Check
summary: A release reviewer compares near-miss decisions for unit, property, replay, headless sdl, render, and playtest evidence and accepts only runnable changed-case evidence, explicit limitations, and named browser, pygame-ce, SDL, display, input, audio, filesystem, packaging, GPU, and production transfer gates. Planned artifact: Record independent decisions and explanations for risk-layered automated and human game test suite. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case pract…
objectives: Test vector, wrap, time, input, state, score, collision, spawn, and migration invariants with fixed fixtures.
Generate bounded cases for wrap, collision symmetry, conservation, reconciliation, replay, and invalid input and retain smallest failures.
Use dummy video and audio drivers for adapter integration while labeling missing window, GPU, timing, device, and sensory evidence.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

starterFiles.python:
"""build-asteroids-python cumulative accessible real-time game portfolio.

Browser execution uses deterministic pure-Python simulation models and fixed fixtures only.
Native pygame-ce, SDL, windows, controllers, audio devices, assets, files, installers,
GPU behavior, frame pacing, accessibility playtests, packaging, and production effects require controlled transfer gates.
"""

from dataclasses import dataclass


@dataclass(frozen=True)
class SimulationPolicy:
    fixed_step_seconds: float = 1 / 120
    max_frame_seconds: float = 0.1
    max_updates_per_frame: int = 8
    logical_width: int = 960
    logical_height: int = 540


@dataclass(frozen=True)
class RunEvidence:
    revision: str
    seed: int
    input_trace_id: str
    runtime_id: str
    accessibility_profile: str


def portfolio_ready() -> str:
    return "Python Asteroids portfolio ready"

steps.title: Asteroids, pygame-ce, and Python Reconstruct the defensible process · Quiz Pure Unit Tests
steps.instruction:
Order the Pure Unit Tests Asteroids, pygame-ce, and Python player-input-to-release evidence cycle for this quiz task before implementing it.

Evidence target: Asteroids, pygame-ce, and Python Reconstruct the defensible process · Quiz Pure Unit Tests.
steps.why: Correct order keeps Pure Unit Tests assumptions visible before execution and verifies its result against a boundary case afterward. For Asteroids, pygame-ce, and Python Reconstruct the defensible process · Quiz Pure Unit Tests, keep Pure Unit Tests connected to executable Python game-state, timing, input, collision, accessibility, test, package, and release decision evidence.

content.text: A quiz Asteroids team handles case 1757 during a low-vision laptop playtest at 125 percent scaling. Build deterministic Python evidence to layer pure unit and property tests, deterministic replay, headless SDL integration, render contracts, changed defects, and manual accessibility playtests; record the first failed invariant, repair, regression evidence, playtest observation, rollback, recovery, and residual-risk owner; then run the packaged build with dummy video and audio drivers, missing optional sound, and a corrupted save. Browser code uses pure bounded models and original fixed fixtures only. Real pygame-ce, SDL, windows, controllers, audio devices, assets, files, installers, frame timing, GPUs, packaging, and production behavior require explicit controlled desktop transfer gates. Competency probe: Test vector, wrap, time, input, state, score, collision, spawn, and migration invariants with fixed fixtures.

steps.title: Asteroids, pygame-ce, and Python Defend one difficult decision · Quiz Playtest Accessibility
steps.instruction:
Write the quiz evidence note for Playtest Accessibility: explain the governing condition, one failure, and how this concrete case was verified — A quiz Asteroids team handles case 2819 during a low-vision laptop playtest at 125 percent scaling. Build deterministic Python evidence to layer pure unit and property tests, deterministic replay, headless SDL integration, render contracts, changed defects, and manual accessibility playtests; test empty, zero-vector, seam, low-frame-rate, focus-loss, device-loss, missing-asset, corrupted-save, and rejected cases; then cross both screen seams in one update and retain collision and rendering continuity. Browser code uses pure bounded models and original fixed fixtures only. Real pygame-ce, SDL, windows, controllers, audio devices, assets, files, installers, frame timing, GPUs, packaging, and production behavior require explicit controlled desktop transfer gates. Competency probe: Run structured tasks with varied players and devices, capture barriers and observations ethically, repair, and retest.
